---
title: Concepts
weight: 2
---

The ideas behind DDx and document-driven development.

## Documents Are the Product

The fundamental shift: **you maintain documents, agents produce code.**

In traditional development, code is the primary artifact. In document-driven development, the primary artifacts are the documents that tell agents what to build and how to build it:

- **Prompts** — instructions that direct agent behavior for specific tasks
- **Personas** — behavioral definitions that shape how agents approach work
- **Patterns** — proven solutions to recurring problems, written for agent consumption
- **Templates** — project and file blueprints with variable substitution
- **Specs** — requirements and designs that define what to build

The quality of agent output follows directly from the quality of these documents. Better documents produce better code, every time.

## The Document Library

DDx organizes agent-facing documents into a structured library:

```
.ddx/library/
├── prompts/        # Agent instructions
├── personas/       # Behavioral definitions
├── patterns/       # Reusable solutions
├── templates/      # Project blueprints
├── configs/        # Tool configurations
├── mcp-servers/    # MCP server registry
└── environments/   # Environment-specific docs
```

These are plain Markdown and YAML files. Any agent can read them. Any developer can edit them. Git tracks every change.

## Composition Over Monoliths

Instead of maintaining one giant instruction set, DDx encourages **small, focused documents combined on demand**:

- A persona defines behavior ("be a strict code reviewer")
- A pattern defines an approach ("handle errors this way")
- A spec defines requirements ("build this feature")

Composed together, they give an agent everything it needs for a specific task. Each piece is independently maintainable and reusable.

## Personas

A persona is a document that shapes how an agent behaves. DDx ships with personas like:

| Persona | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| `code-reviewer` | Pedantic about quality, catches edge cases, demands tests |
| `implementer` | Ships working code fast, avoids over-engineering |
| `test-engineer` | Writes tests first, validates thoroughly |
| `architect` | Chooses the simplest design that works |

You **bind** personas to **roles** in your project configuration:

```yaml
# .ddx.yml
persona_bindings:
  code-reviewer: code-reviewer
  architect: architect
```

When an agent is assigned to a role, it picks up the bound persona and adjusts its approach.

## Git-Native Sync

DDx uses git subtree to synchronize document libraries:

- **Pull** community improvements into your project
- **Push** your improvements back to the upstream library
- **Override** specific documents locally without losing sync

No external services. No lock-in. Standard git workflows.

## Infrastructure, Not Methodology

DDx is deliberately **general and abstract**. It provides primitives:

- Document library management
- Persona system with role bindings
- Template engine with variables
- Git-based sync
- Meta-prompt injection
- MCP server management

Specific development methodologies — how to structure your development process, what phases to follow, what gates to enforce — are built as **separate tools on top of DDx**. DDx provides the foundation; workflow tools provide the opinions.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-53c96967
Title:    epic: refine vision + principles, expand microsite docs, generate visuals
Type:     epic
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   helix, phase:frame, kind:epic, area:docs
Desc:     Three-part epic on the guiding artifacts:

1. Tighten the frame: refine docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md and docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md. Sharpen, prune to load-bearing items, surface decisions that have settled (cost-tiered work, endpoint-first routing, project-local installs, single-skill consolidation).

2. Expand microsite concept docs: port the refined frame into website/content/docs/concepts/ (or update what exists). Audience is users discovering DDx — needs to be clearer than the internal frame, with examples.

3. Visuals: produce SVG/Mermaid diagrams (three-layer DDx/HELIX/Dun stack, bead lifecycle, execute-loop, persona binding) and at least one polished hero graphic via nano-banana-pro-openrouter for the microsite landing page.

Children:
- V1: Refine vision + principles (frame docs, in-place)
- V2: Microsite concept docs (depends on V1)
- V3: Visuals — diagrams + hero graphics (depends on V1)

Constraint: REFINE the existing docs, do not full-rewrite. Preserve voice and existing structure where it works.
Accept:   All 3 child beads closed with passing AC. End-user experience: a fresh visitor to docs.ddx.dev can read the concepts pages start-to-finish and understand what DDx is, what it isn't, and how it relates to HELIX/Dun. Hero graphic visible on landing page.
Created:  2026-04-29 15:23:45
Updated:  2026-04-29 15:23:45
---V1---
ID:       ddx-c5dce902
Title:    refine vision + principles (in-place sharpening of frame docs)
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   helix, phase:frame, kind:docs, area:docs
Parent:   ddx-53c96967
Desc:     V1 of 3 children under epic ddx-53c96967.

REFINE (do not rewrite) docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md and docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md to reflect what's settled.

CURRENT STATE:
- product-vision.md (177 lines) — written before several decisions landed. Has aspirational language and outdated references.
- principles.md (72 lines) — short list of guiding principles. Some are load-bearing, some are filler.
- prd.md (370 lines) and concerns.md (257 lines) are reference context — read for background, but DO NOT modify them in this bead.

DECISIONS THAT HAVE SETTLED AND SHOULD BE REFLECTED:
1. DDx is the platform layer; HELIX is the workflow layer; Dun is the quality layer. Three-project stack with explicit boundaries.
2. Project-local everything: ddx init/install only touch <projectRoot>. The only global ddx artifact is ddx-server. (FEAT-015, just shipped.)
3. Cost-tiered work: cheap models do, strong models review, deterministic fixes at the top of the ladder. Goal is closed-bead throughput per dollar.
4. Endpoint-first routing redesign: providers are endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match (in progress; reference don't promise).
5. Single-skill consolidation: DDx ships ONE 'ddx' skill (not seven). Per FEAT-011.
6. Bead-driven work: beads are the unit of work; execute-loop drains the queue with auto-recovery and review.

REFINEMENT GOALS:
- Sharpen: every sentence load-bearing. Cut filler.
- Prune principles to ones that drive actual decisions. If a principle isn't cited in any spec, remove it.
- Surface the three-layer architecture early in the vision.
- Make the 'platform vs workflow' boundary explicit.
- Voice: confident, technical, concise. No marketing fluff.

CONSTRAINT: in-place edits via Edit tool. Preserve sections that work. Do not full-rewrite. Do not change frontmatter/IDs.
Accept:   1. docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md updated; net length within +/- 30% of current 177 lines. 2. docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md updated; principles list reduced to load-bearing items only. 3. Three-layer architecture (DDx/HELIX/Dun) introduced in vision overview. 4. 'Platform vs workflow' boundary explicit in vision and principles. 5. The 6 settled decisions (project-local, cost-tiered, endpoint-first, single-skill, bead-driven, three-layer) are reflected — not necessarily by name, but the underlying ideas are present and consistent. 6. No filler, no marketing language, no aspirational features that haven't shipped. 7. Frontmatter and HELIX IDs preserved.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 720 420" font-family="ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif" font-size="14">
  <title>The DDx / HELIX / Dun three-layer stack</title>
  <desc>DDx is the platform layer. HELIX is the workflow layer that consumes DDx primitives. Dun is the quality layer that runs checks against the working tree.</desc>

  <!-- Workflow layer -->
  <rect x="40" y="40" width="640" height="90" rx="10" fill="#e9f1fb" stroke="#4878c6" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="60" y="70" font-weight="700" fill="#1f3a68">HELIX (workflow layer)</text>
  <text x="60" y="92" fill="#1f3a68">phases · gates · supervisory dispatch · methodology-specific validation</text>
  <text x="60" y="112" fill="#1f3a68" font-style="italic" font-size="12">consumes: beads, personas, agent dispatch, templates, document library</text>

  <!-- Platform layer -->
  <rect x="40" y="160" width="640" height="120" rx="10" fill="#eaf6ee" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="60" y="190" font-weight="700" fill="#1c5a31">DDx (platform layer)</text>
  <text x="60" y="212" fill="#1c5a31">document library · bead tracker · agent dispatch · personas · templates · git sync · MCP</text>
  <g fill="#fff" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="1.5">
    <rect x="60"  y="226" width="120" height="38" rx="6"/>
    <rect x="190" y="226" width="120" height="38" rx="6"/>
    <rect x="320" y="226" width="120" height="38" rx="6"/>
    <rect x="450" y="226" width="100" height="38" rx="6"/>
    <rect x="560" y="226" width="100" height="38" rx="6"/>
  </g>
  <g fill="#1c5a31" text-anchor="middle">
    <text x="120" y="250">beads</text>
    <text x="250" y="250">personas</text>
    <text x="380" y="250">agent dispatch</text>
    <text x="500" y="250">templates</text>
    <text x="610" y="250">git sync</text>
  </g>

  <!-- Quality layer -->
  <rect x="40" y="310" width="640" height="80" rx="10" fill="#f5ecf7" stroke="#8e35a3" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="60" y="340" font-weight="700" fill="#5b1f6a">Dun (quality layer)</text>
  <text x="60" y="362" fill="#5b1f6a">check discovery · execution · agent-friendly output</text>
  <text x="60" y="382" fill="#5b1f6a" font-style="italic" font-size="12">consumed by: HELIX gates, CI, ad-hoc developer runs</text>

  <!-- Arrows: workflow consumes platform -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#1f3a68" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#a1)">
    <path d="M360 130 L360 158"/>
  </g>
  <g fill="none" stroke="#5b1f6a" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#a2)">
    <path d="M360 308 L360 282"/>
  </g>

  <defs>
    <marker id="a1" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0 0 L10 5 L0 10 z" fill="#1f3a68"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="a2" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0 0 L10 5 L0 10 z" fill="#5b1f6a"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>
</svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 820 360" font-family="ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif" font-size="13">
  <title>Bead lifecycle</title>
  <desc>A bead moves from open to ready when its dependencies are satisfied, then to in_progress when claimed, then to closed when the work merges. Review can reopen a bead; push conflicts auto-recover; declined work returns to ready.</desc>

  <defs>
    <marker id="ar" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0 0 L10 5 L0 10 z" fill="#333"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arRed" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0 0 L10 5 L0 10 z" fill="#b3361b"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- States -->
  <g font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle">
    <g>
      <rect x="40"  y="140" width="120" height="60" rx="30" fill="#f4f6fa" stroke="#5a6a85" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="100" y="175">open</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="220" y="140" width="120" height="60" rx="30" fill="#e3eefc" stroke="#3b6db8" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="280" y="175">ready</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="400" y="140" width="140" height="60" rx="30" fill="#fff4dc" stroke="#c9942a" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="470" y="175">in_progress</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="620" y="140" width="140" height="60" rx="30" fill="#dff3e1" stroke="#2e7a3a" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="690" y="175">closed</text>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Forward arrows -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.6" marker-end="url(#ar)">
    <path d="M160 170 L218 170"/>
    <path d="M340 170 L398 170"/>
    <path d="M540 170 L618 170"/>
  </g>
  <g text-anchor="middle" fill="#333" font-size="12">
    <text x="189" y="160">deps met</text>
    <text x="369" y="160">claim</text>
    <text x="579" y="160">merge ok</text>
  </g>

  <!-- Branches: review reopens -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#b3361b" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-dasharray="5,4" marker-end="url(#arRed)">
    <path d="M540 200 C 500 280, 320 280, 280 202"/>
    <path d="M540 140 C 600 70, 700 70, 720 138"/>
  </g>
  <g fill="#b3361b" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">
    <text x="410" y="276">review reopens (escalate model)</text>
    <text x="630" y="80">push conflict → auto-recover</text>
  </g>

  <!-- Declined -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#7a3aa6" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-dasharray="3,3" marker-end="url(#ar)">
    <path d="M470 200 C 470 250, 320 240, 280 202"/>
  </g>
  <text x="380" y="240" fill="#7a3aa6" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">declined / no_changes → re-queue</text>

  <!-- Title -->
  <text x="410" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" font-size="16" fill="#222">Bead lifecycle</text>
  <text x="410" y="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555" font-size="12">solid = forward · dashed = recovery / re-queue</text>
</svg>

data.output: File created successfully at: /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-5028c8e6-20260429T163247-2c476bc4/website/static/diagrams/bead-lifecycle.svg

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 860 380" font-family="ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif" font-size="13">
  <title>Execute-loop flow</title>
  <desc>The execute-loop drains the bead queue: claim a ready bead, render the prompt, run the agent in an isolated worktree, review the result, merge or push (with auto-recovery on race), then close the bead.</desc>

  <defs>
    <marker id="el" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0 0 L10 5 L0 10 z" fill="#333"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <text x="430" y="34" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" font-size="16">Execute-loop</text>
  <text x="430" y="54" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">ddx work · drains the bead queue end-to-end</text>

  <!-- Steps in a row -->
  <g font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle">
    <g>
      <rect x="30"  y="130" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#eaf6ee" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="90" y="158">claim</text>
      <text x="90" y="178" font-weight="400" font-size="11" fill="#1c5a31">ready bead</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="180" y="130" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#eaf6ee" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="240" y="158">render</text>
      <text x="240" y="178" font-weight="400" font-size="11" fill="#1c5a31">prompt + context</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="330" y="130" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#eaf6ee" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="390" y="158">run agent</text>
      <text x="390" y="178" font-weight="400" font-size="11" fill="#1c5a31">isolated worktree</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="480" y="130" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#fff4dc" stroke="#c9942a" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="540" y="158">review</text>
      <text x="540" y="178" font-weight="400" font-size="11" fill="#7a5a13">stronger model</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="630" y="130" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#e3eefc" stroke="#3b6db8" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="690" y="158">merge / push</text>
      <text x="690" y="178" font-weight="400" font-size="11" fill="#1f3a68">auto-recover on race</text>
    </g>
    <g>
      <rect x="780" y="130" width="60" height="60" rx="8" fill="#dff3e1" stroke="#2e7a3a" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="810" y="165">close</text>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Forward arrows -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.6" marker-end="url(#el)">
    <path d="M150 160 L178 160"/>
    <path d="M300 160 L328 160"/>
    <path d="M450 160 L478 160"/>
    <path d="M600 160 L628 160"/>
    <path d="M750 160 L778 160"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Loop back -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#666" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-dasharray="5,4" marker-end="url(#el)">
    <path d="M810 190 C 810 280, 90 290, 90 192"/>
  </g>
  <text x="450" y="290" text-anchor="middle" fill="#444" font-size="12">next ready bead</text>

  <!-- Branch from review -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#b3361b" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-dasharray="4,3" marker-end="url(#el)">
    <path d="M540 130 C 540 80, 390 80, 390 128"/>
  </g>
  <text x="465" y="76" fill="#b3361b" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">findings → re-run</text>

  <!-- Bottom note -->
  <text x="430" y="350" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555" font-size="12">cheap models do · stronger models review · deterministic fixes at the top of the ladder</text>
</svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 800 380" font-family="ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif" font-size="13">
  <title>Persona / role binding</title>
  <desc>A workflow declares abstract roles. The DDx library defines reusable personas. The project's .ddx/config.yaml binds roles to personas; agents pick up the bound persona at dispatch time.</desc>

  <defs>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 780 380" font-family="ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif" font-size="13">
  <title>Project-local install model</title>
  <desc>ddx init and ddx install only write under the project root: .ddx/plugins, .agents/skills, .claude/skills. The only global ddx artifact is ddx-server.</desc>

  <text x="390" y="32" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" font-size="16">Project-local install model</text>
  <text x="390" y="52" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555" font-size="12">ddx init / ddx install only touch <projectRoot></text>

  <!-- Project root box -->
  <rect x="40" y="80" width="500" height="270" rx="10" fill="#eaf6ee" stroke="#35a35f" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="60" y="106" font-weight="700" fill="#1c5a31"><projectRoot>/</text>

  <g font-family="ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, monospace" font-size="13" fill="#1c5a31">
    <text x="80" y="140">.ddx/</text>
    <text x="100" y="160">plugins/</text>
    <text x="120" y="180">ddx/         <tspan fill="#555">← default plugin</tspan></text>
    <text x="120" y="200">helix/       <tspan fill="#555">← installed workflow</tspan></text>
    <text x="120" y="220">…</text>
    <text x="100" y="240">config.yaml  <tspan fill="#555">← persona bindings, providers</tspan></text>

    <text x="80" y="270">.agents/skills/  <tspan fill="#555">← real files (not symlinks)</tspan></text>
    <text x="80" y="296">.claude/skills/  <tspan fill="#555">← real files (not symlinks)</tspan></text>

    <text x="80" y="326">src/, package.json, …  <tspan fill="#555">← your code</tspan></text>
  </g>

  <!-- Global box -->
  <rect x="580" y="80" width="170" height="120" rx="10" fill="#e9f1fb" stroke="#4878c6" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="665" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" fill="#1f3a68">global</text>
  <text x="665" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#1f3a68">(only artifact)</text>
  <text x="665" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-family="ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, monospace" fill="#1f3a68">ddx-server</text>
  <text x="665" y="182" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#1f3a68">long-running daemon</text>

  <!-- Anti box -->
  <rect x="580" y="220" width="170" height="130" rx="10" fill="#fbeaea" stroke="#b3361b" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
  <text x="665" y="248" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" fill="#7a1c0d">retired</text>
  <g font-family="ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, monospace" font-size="11" fill="#7a1c0d" text-anchor="middle">
    <text x="665" y="276">~/.ddx/...</text>
    <text x="665" y="296">~/.claude/skills/*</text>
    <text x="665" y="316">ddx install --global</text>
  </g>
  <text x="665" y="340" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#7a1c0d">no shared mutable state</text>
</svg>

input.old_string: The ideas behind DDx and document-driven development.
input.new_string:
The ideas behind DDx and document-driven development.

## The Three-Layer Stack

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack. The platform stays general; opinions live in workflow tools; quality checks live in their own runner.

![DDx / HELIX / Dun three-layer stack](/diagrams/three-layer-stack.svg)

- **DDx** — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync, MCP.
- **HELIX** — a workflow methodology built on DDx. Phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, methodology-specific validation.
- **Dun** — a quality runner. Discovers and runs checks; emits agent-friendly output.

DDx provides primitives. HELIX (and others) provide opinions. Dun verifies the result.

input.old_string: The ideas behind DDx and document-driven development.
input.new_string:
The ideas behind DDx and document-driven development.

## The Three-Layer Stack

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack. The platform stays general; opinions live in workflow tools; quality checks live in their own runner.

![DDx / HELIX / Dun three-layer stack](/diagrams/three-layer-stack.svg)

- **DDx** — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync, MCP.
- **HELIX** — a workflow methodology built on DDx. Phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, methodology-specific validation.
- **Dun** — a quality runner. Discovers and runs checks; emits agent-friendly output.

DDx provides primitives. HELIX (and others) provide opinions. Dun verifies the result.

data.text: Now add the rest of the diagrams to the concepts page (bead lifecycle, execute-loop, persona binding, project-local install):

input.old_string: When an agent is assigned to a role, it picks up the bound persona and adjusts its approach.
input.new_string:
When an agent is assigned to a role, it picks up the bound persona and adjusts its approach.

![Persona / role binding](/diagrams/persona-binding.svg)

## Beads: the Unit of Work

Every task is a **bead**. Beads carry a description, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and a status. Agents claim ready beads, do the work, and close them. The queue drives what happens next.

![Bead lifecycle](/diagrams/bead-lifecycle.svg)

The execute-loop drains the queue end-to-end: claim a ready bead, render its prompt, run the agent in an isolated worktree, review the result, merge or push (with auto-recovery on race), and close.

![Execute-loop flow](/diagrams/execute-loop.svg)

Cheap models do the work; stronger models review; deterministic fixes sit at the top of the ladder. The metric is closed-bead throughput per dollar.

## Project-Local Everything

`ddx init` and `ddx install` only touch the project root. There is no shared mutable state outside the repo. The single global artifact is `ddx-server` — a long-running daemon — and it is optional.

![Project-local install model](/diagrams/project-local-install.svg)

input.content:
# Landing hero — blocked: OPENROUTER_API_KEY unavailable

This bead (ddx-5028c8e6, V3 of epic ddx-53c96967) was instructed to generate
a polished hero graphic for the microsite landing page using the
`nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill. That skill calls OpenRouter and requires
`OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in the environment.

At execution time:
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` was not set in the environment or in any `.env` the
  worktree could read.
- The `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill was not installed in `~/.claude/skills/`
  or `.claude/skills/`.

Per the bead's AC #3 contingency clause, the hero portion is declined with this
documented blocker note. The SVG diagrams (AC #1, #2) are committed; the
landing-page integration point is wired and ready for the image (AC #5 — the
hero, when generated, must be distinctive, not generic AI clip art).

## Prompt for nano-banana-pro-openrouter

When the API key is available, run the skill with this prompt:

> A flat, editorial-illustration-style hero image for "DDx — the platform for
> document-driven development." Visual metaphor: a structured tree of crisp
> Markdown documents (headings, bullets, code fences visible) flowing as a
> directed graph into a constellation of small autonomous agents that emit
> green-checkmark commits on the right. The documents are the source of
> truth; the agents are downstream. Avoid: glowing brains, robot heads,
> generic blue circuit boards, neural-net swirls, "AI" in any glow effect.
> Aesthetic: muted teal/green/amber palette on off-white, thin 1.5px line
> work, occasional flat-color fills, subtle paper grain, generous negative
> space. Composition: left-to-right reading order, documents on the left,
> agents on the right, the graph edges labelled with tiny tag chips
> ("persona", "spec", "pattern"). 2K resolution minimum, 16:9, no text
> rendered into the image (we'll set the headline in HTML).

Generate 3–4 variants; pick the strongest by these criteria:
1. Documents are unambiguously the **source**, agents the **consumer**.
2. No generic AI imagery (brains, robots, glowing nodes).
3. Reads as editorial illustration, not stock-photo collage.
4. Works on both light and dark backgrounds (or supply two variants).

## Integration point

Save the chosen variant as one of:
- `website/static/hero/landing.webp` (preferred — smaller)
- `website/static/hero/landing.png` (fallback)

Then wire it into the landing hero by editing `website/content/_index.md`.
A ready-to-uncomment block has been left there (search for
`HERO_GRAPHIC_PLACEHOLDER`). Uncomment it once the asset exists.

## Follow-up

Reopen ddx-5028c8e6 (or open a follow-up bead) once `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is
available in the execution environment. The diagrams below remain valid:

- `website/static/diagrams/three-layer-stack.svg`
- `website/static/diagrams/bead-lifecycle.svg`
- `website/static/diagrams/execute-loop.svg`
- `website/static/diagrams/persona-binding.svg`
- `website/static/diagrams/project-local-install.svg`

data.text:
Committed `7647db6b`. AC summary:

1. **Diagrams (AC#1)** — 5 SVGs at `website/static/diagrams/`: three-layer-stack, bead-lifecycle, execute-loop, persona-binding, project-local-install.
2. **Placeholders filled (AC#2)** — V2 hadn't run yet, so I wired diagrams directly into the existing `website/content/docs/concepts/_index.md` (5 references confirmed in rendered HTML).
3. **Hero (AC#3)** — declined per contingency clause: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` not set, nano-banana skill not installed. Prompt + selection criteria + integration point recorded in `website/static/hero/HERO_BLOCKER.md`; `HERO_GRAPHIC_PLACEHOLDER` block left in `website/content/_index.md` ready to uncomment. Bead notes updated with blocker.
4. **Hugo build (AC#4)** — passes: 140 pages, 30 static files, 0 errors.
5. **No generic AI clip art (AC#5)** — no image was committed; the placeholder explicitly forbids generic AI imagery in the prompt.
Bead complete. Committed `7647db6b` with 5 SVG diagrams, concepts-page wiring, hero blocker note, and a ready-to-uncomment hero placeholder in the landing page. Hugo build passed.
